summary: Validated all new improvements on top of Seed Recovery Dashboard. Backend: 9/9 new pytest cases pass (estimate, presets, persisted NDJSON logs, WebSocket stream incl. snapshot + log events + clean close on unknown job). Orphan-cleanup verified end-to-end by inserting a fake 'running' job in Mongo and restarting the backend (status correctly flipped to 'failed' with the orphaned error message). Frontend (E2E via Playwright on the public preview URL): pre-flight estimate panel renders feasibility badge (FAST), recalc button triggers re-computation, wordlist preset dropdown lists 16 presets incl. bip39-en (2048), loading bip39-en updates the textarea+count to 2048, starting a real job shows 'WS · LIVE' in the header and 'WS LIVE · N LINES' in the terminal title with streaming output. Regression: 12/14 previous pytest tests still pass. The 2 failures (test_job_logs_stream, test_job_get_status) are caused by the test deadline (45 s) being too short now that the previous 'premature not_found' bug is FIXED — backend correctly waits for proc.wait() through all 4 phases (~110 s). I extended that deadline to 180 s in the test file; the underlying behavior is now correct (NOT_FOUND only after process exit code 0, Stop button disabled, finished_at populated).

minor.issue: search_space_approx is returned as a string ('1.638e+04') even for finite values; clients may want a number. Not a functional bug.
Default queue maxsize=1000; under very chatty processes back-pressure silently drops events (catches asyncio.QueueFull). Acceptable for seedrecover. Consider logging dropped count.
Reads & line-counts every preset file on each call; cache at startup similar to SEEDRECOVER_VERSION for a tiny win.

issues: When wordlist size input is set to a small number (e.g. 4 from a previous custom wordlist), ETA appears '<1s' which is correct math but may mislead users — consider using the loaded candidate-wordlist count as the default rather than 2048 only when no preset is selected.

action_items: (Optional) Cache GET /api/wordlists/presets results in memory at startup; iterate over files only once.
(Optional) Return numeric search_space (not string) from /api/jobs/estimate for easier client-side display & sorting.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:863 cleanup_orphans is registered with @app.on_event('startup'); FastAPI is moving these to lifespan handlers. Works on current FastAPI but consider migrating to the lifespan context manager.

server.py:817 ws_job_stream lives at app root, not on /api router. Path '/api/jobs/{id}/stream' makes the ingress route correctly but bypassing the APIRouter means it won't appear in OpenAPI docs and global API middleware (if any added later) may not apply.

server.py:680 search-space computation uses 10**log_combos with log_combos<18 guard; threshold is somewhat arbitrary — consider rejecting +/- inf and explicitly returning JSON-safe 'inf' string already done, OK.
server.py:196 broadcast loop iterates over runtime.subscribers and uses put_nowait (non-blocking) — good. But on QueueFull there is no logging; under heavy traffic UI will silently miss lines.
server.py:203 load_logs_from_disk is sync I/O on the event loop; for very large NDJSON files this could block. Wrap with asyncio.to_thread or aiofiles.
server.py:393 status decision relies on `_found_hit` flag set inside the stream reader — good fix for prior premature_not_found bug; verified working in this iteration.

success_rate.backend: 21/23 pytest (9/9 new + 12/14 regression; 2 regression timeouts fixed by widening deadline)
success_rate.frontend: 100% — all new panels, indicators, and flows verified

seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_-labeled jobs; orphan-test inserted TEST_orphan_<uuid> and cleaned up via DELETE.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All improvements work as specified. The previous HIGH bug (premature not_found) is fully fixed in run_job() — status is now committed only after proc.wait(). NDJSON logs are written to /app/backend/data/{job_id}/output.ndjson and load_logs_from_disk falls back to them when JOBS in-memory entry is gone. WebSocket endpoint is /api/jobs/{id}/stream at app root (not under /api router) — works via ingress. Orphan cleanup runs at startup and marks running/pending jobs with no in-memory runtime as failed. The 2 regression failures in backend_test.py were timeout-related and were fixed by widening the deadline.
rca of the issue: Regression test_job_logs_stream / test_job_get_status timed out at 45 s because the iteration_1 bug (premature 'not_found' set on first phase log line) was fixed by deferring status finalization until after proc.wait(). Real seedrecover runs 4 phases serially, total ~110 s on this pod with threads=1. Test deadline updated to 180 s — underlying app behavior is correct (status='not_found', finished_at populated, Stop button disabled, [process exit code=0] visible in terminal).
